ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Garuda EX' (EX suffix denotes named character in FFTCG) and canonical visual match — pale winged humanoid with feathered headdress matches Garuda's established summon design from FFXI onward. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: Garuda EX (8-051C) is a Wind-element Summon from FFTCG Opus VIII, released March 22, 2019. Opus VIII contains 148 cards (60 Common, 36 Rare, 26 Hero, 14 Legend) with heavy FFXIV and FFXI Primal representation. [Square Enix FFTCG Opus VIII release page]

Garuda first appeared as a named enemy in Final Fantasy III and was elevated to Wind-elemental summon status in Final Fantasy XI — the first time no established Wind summon existed. Her FFXI design as a pale winged humanoid became the visual template for FFXIV, XV, and XVI versions. [CBR: Every Garuda Appearance; Game Rant]

In Final Fantasy XIV, Garuda carries the title 'Lady of the Vortex' and is a Primal worshipped by the Ixal beast tribe, distinguished by her intense hatred of 'landwalkers.' [Final Fantasy Wiki: Garuda (FFXIV)]

The EX designation in FFTCG means the card's ability fires as an EX BURST trigger — unpreventable — when flipped from the Break Zone during damage resolution. This gives Common Summons like Garuda EX competitive relevance beyond base summon effects. [Square Enix FFTCG official FAQ]
